concept: opticurve: true Bezier optimization vs raw polygon
source_excerpt: int opticurve;       /* use curve optimization? */   ...   1,   /* opticurve */   ...   int opticurve;    /* opticurve capable (true Bezier curves?) */
implication: opticurve toggles whether potrace fits true optimized Bezier curves (on, default) or leaves the un-optimized polygon (off). Bezier output is smoother and lighter; polygon output preserves every vertex faithfully but is heavier and more angular. This is a mode-level choice, not a scalar.

param_mapping.rationale: vtracer mode='spline' fits smooth Bezier splines (analogue of potrace opticurve ON); mode='polygon' emits straight-segment polygons (opticurve OFF). Direct conceptual match. Default spline for smooth art; polygon worth testing on hard-edged geometric/text art and soft-shaded sources (vectorizer already adds a soft_polygon_max start).

hypothesis: mode='spline' (Bezier, opticurve on) will beat polygon on ssim for curved/organic art, while mode='polygon' may match or beat spline on angular/geometric art and gradient region tracing, because it mirrors potrace's opticurve Bezier-vs-polygon distinction.
